newsSince: No new fundamental news since June 9. The record fiscal-Q3 print, the WFE-market raise to ~$140B, and the >50% advanced-packaging growth outlook were the April 22 earnings, already in the prior case. The +14% move since June 9 is a string of analyst price-target raises (Barclays and others) and AI-capex momentum, not a fresh thesis shift; China remains ~34% of revenue.

sources.label: Lam Q3 FY26 results, WFE raised to $140B
New US-China chip export curbs impact on LRCX
Lam WFE outlook may be bigger than expected ($140B)

verdicts.rankRationale: AI-node bottleneck lens: etch/deposition centrality to AI fab + HBM confirmed by the $140B WFE raise, but export curbs hit the exact China risk the thesis flags. Both predate June 9 and roughly offset. No new node-criticality info, so hold.
verdicts.bull: Mid-30s% SAM share; indispensable to advanced-node and HBM capacity adds.
verdicts.neutral: Node-critical but cyclical and export-exposed; valuation rich after +102% YTD.
verdicts.bear: ~34% China revenue under tightening curbs; WFE cyclicality.

verdicts.rankRationale: CPO lens: only an indirect beneficiary via HBM/foundry/advanced-interconnect capex. June 9 already cut it for crowding (+102% YTD, ~$466B cap) and export drag. No new CPO-relevant evidence since; residual asymmetry stays thin but a second consecutive demotion isn't warranted.
verdicts.bull: Advanced-packaging/interconnect capex tailwind as HBM scales.
verdicts.neutral: Indirect CPO read-through; mega-cap with little hidden alpha.
verdicts.bear: Not a CPO bottleneck; crowded, export-exposed.

verdicts.rankRationale: Under-owned-bottleneck master list, fab-tools bucket. June 9 already demoted for the YTD rerating; the only genuinely new items (WFE $140B positive vs. China curb negative) offset, so no second consecutive demotion. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Pick-and-shovel exposure to all leading-edge and HBM capacity.
verdicts.neutral: Strong franchise, fully recognized after the run.
